title: Add rival-relative undercut/overcut pressure features from same-lap field context
summary: Public F1 strategy explanations emphasize that pit calls depend on undercut/overcut risk, traffic, and being within striking range of rivals, not only absolute tyre age.
rationale: Public F1 strategy explanations emphasize that pit calls depend on undercut/overcut risk, traffic, and being within striking range of rivals, not only absolute tyre age. Current best features are mostly single-car features.
implementation_hint: Within each Race+Lap group, create relative features: position-normalized rank, nearest rival ahead/behind, counts of rivals within +/-2 positions, percentile rank of LapTime_Delta and degradation, compound mismatch versus nearby cars, and proxies for clean-air or traffic after a stop.
expected_effect: Should capture strategic pressure that triggers reactive stops, especially in midfield packs where absolute tyre wear alone is insufficient.
risk: The dataset lacks true time gaps, so position-based traffic proxies may be noisy or track-dependent.
